rows.title: Multi-Turn Jailbreaking of Aligned LLMs via Lexical Anchor Tree Search
rows.note: Supports the claim that multi-turn conversational structure is a meaningful attack surface.

rows.title: ICON: Intent-Context Coupling for Efficient Multi-Turn Jailbreak Attack
rows.note: Supports context-sensitive safety degradation under authoritative or congruent framing.

rows.title: Structured Semantic Cloaking for Jailbreak Attacks on Large Language Models
rows.note: Most directly supports the core stress condition used in this project.

rows.title: Staged Direct multi-turn control
rows.note: This is a project-side control family added to disambiguate turn count from cloaking structure.

rows.title: Stealthy Jailbreaking Attacks via Hyperbolic Hamiltonian Dynamics and Mobius Fusion
rows.note: Useful only as optional robustness context; not needed for the core claim.

rows.title: TAO-Attack: Toward Advanced Optimization-Based Jailbreak Attacks for Large Language Models
rows.note: Provides attack landscape context, not direct support for temporal lag measurement.

rule: No manuscript claim is kept without an exact source mapping and support-level review.
